name: reduction_48
strategy: Same two cuBLAS GEMMs as the reference (bit-identical), but replace the reference's ~9 separate fp32 elementwise ops for

sources.content:
import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F


@torch.no_grad()
def run(x: torch.Tensor, gate_proj: torch.Tensor, up_proj: torch.Tensor) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Fused gate and up projection with GELU-tanh activation.

    Computes: gelu_tanh(x @ gate_proj.T) * (x @ up_proj.T)

    Same two cuBLAS GEMMs as the reference (bit-identical math, zero numerical
    risk), but the reference's ~9 separate fp32 elementwise ops for the
    GELU-tanh chain (to(f32), pow, mul, add, mul, tanh, add, mul, to(bf16))
    collapse into ONE native fused ATen kernel (`F.gelu(..., approximate=
    "tanh")`, which internally upcasts to fp32 for the tanh math exactly like
    the reference and downcasts back to bf16), followed by a single multiply.
    That takes the epilogue from ~9 kernel launches (each paying real B200
    per-kernel device-time overhead on top of a wasteful fp32-width
    intermediate) down to 2, without introducing any custom kernel code.
    """
    gate_output = torch.matmul(x, gate_proj.t())
    up_output = torch.matmul(x, up_proj.t())

    activated_gate = F.gelu(gate_output, approximate="tanh")
    output = activated_gate.mul(up_output)

    return output
